recent_calls.summary: The agent greeted the user and attempted to connect them with a live representative. The call was quickly transferred to another agent for further assistance.
The user called Budget customer service, provided their ZIP code 63122, and requested to speak with a live agent. The call was then transferred successfully to another agent for further assistance.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer promptly.

The user inquired whether their estimate or work order was sent to American Home Shield. The agent informed the user that they do not have real-time access but will forward the request to the team for follow-up. The call ended with the user declining further assistance.
The agent greeted the user and attempted to engage in conversation, but the user did not respond and ended the call shortly after.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer promptly.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user, Flint Couse, called to schedule an appointment for AC unit service due to a suspected Freon issue. The agent successfully booked a provisional appointment for Monday at 2 PM and collected feedback from the user, who rated the experience positively. The call concluded with the user satisfied and no further issues.

Donna called to set up an appointment for air conditioner service under her American Home Shield home warranty. She scheduled a morning appointment for Tuesday after the weekend and will provide the dispatch number later. The agent confirmed the details and offered information about maintenance programs, which Donna declined, and she rated the service experience positively.

Demarcus called to schedule a heating and cooling service appointment through American Home Shield for the afternoon of June 11th. After some delay in locating the dispatch number, he provided it, and the agent confirmed the details before ending the call. Demarcus declined information about maintenance programs and gave positive feedback about the assistance received.

The user, Christopher Ross, scheduled a plumbing service appointment for a cartridge replacement at his home in Ballwin, Missouri. The agent confirmed the earliest available time on May 25th at 10 AM and arranged to email the estimate invoice to the user. The call concluded with the user declining to participate in a feedback survey.
The user requested to speak to a live person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to another live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent who could assist further.

The call involved a discussion about a dispatch for Harold and Marillie Salzer, where the user requested to proceed with the replacement option for the whole unit instead of just the compressor repair. The agent confirmed the request would be passed along and mentioned potential delays due to the holiday. The call ended with the agent transferring the user to a live agent for further assistance.
The user called to speak with a live agent and was promptly transferred to one by the initial agent. The call lasted 14 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and completed the transfer successfully.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and completed the transfer successfully.

The user called and was greeted by the agent Sophie, who attempted to assist but quickly transferred the call to a live agent for further help. The call lasted 13 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.

Alex from American Home Shield called on behalf of a member regarding an ongoing service issue where the homeowner cannot turn on the unit because the technician removed all the Freon during the last visit. The agent acknowledged the issue, confirmed the service address and contact details, and assured that the team would follow up promptly to arrange a technician visit, preferably the same day. The call ended with the agent confirming no further assistance was needed.
The user called Budget for HVAC service in Illinois and mentioned flooding concerns. The agent assessed the situation and transferred the call to a live representative for further assistance.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the initial agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

The user called to inquire about a voicemail they received and whether they were scheduled for an appointment on Tuesday. The agent explained they couldn't access the voicemail or live schedule but noted the user's request for a follow-up. The call ended with the agent confirming the team will contact the user on the next business day.

Linda called after hours requesting immediate assistance due to flooding in her basement. The agent explained that no live representative was available but assured her that the emergency was documented and the team would follow up on the next business day. Linda inquired about an emergency number and confirmed her service address during the call.
The call was very brief and disconnected shortly after it started, with the user not clearly communicating and hanging up quickly.

Linda called after hours to report a flooding emergency and requested an emergency contact number. The agent explained that no live assistance or dispatch was available after hours but assured that the issue was documented and the team would follow up on the next business day. The call ended with the user hanging up before further assistance could be offered.

Danny Ferreira called to report flooding in the basement caused by the air conditioning system and requested urgent technician assistance. The agent acknowledged the urgency but explained that after-hours immediate service was not available and assured that the issue would be prioritized for the next business day. The agent collected contact and service address information and confirmed a callback would be made.

Karen and Eugene called to report that no one showed up for their scheduled Freon service appointment between 2:30 and 4:30 PM. The agent apologized, documented the missed appointment and their availability, and informed them that a representative would call back on the next business day. The users expressed disappointment with the service and mixed messages received.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to another live agent successfully. The call lasted 13 seconds and ended with a call transfer.

James called to report a problem with a blower motor that was recently installed and is making a weird sound. The agent confirmed the updated service address and assured James that the team would be notified to send someone out as soon as possible. The call ended with the user expressing thanks and no further questions.

The user, Joanne O'Neil, called to inquire about the cost and service charge for replacing a kitchen faucet that was previously replaced by the company and is under warranty. The agent informed her that they do not have direct access to billing information and transferred the call to the billing team for further assistance.
The user, Harold, called to reach CJ after multiple attempts earlier in the day. The agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent who could assist further.

The user called to follow up on air conditioning and furnace cleaning, mentioning a previously scheduled appointment that did not occur. The agent attempted to assist but transferred the call to a live agent for further help. The call ended with a successful transfer.
The user called to return a call to CJ and was transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The agent attempted to connect the user to the appropriate person successfully.
The user returned a call from CJ and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The call lasted 14 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.

The user called to schedule an appointment for cooling system service under a home warranty but did not have the dispatch number. The agent confirmed the details and informed the user that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact them shortly. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

The user called to inquire about the cost of having a technician inspect a water heater. The agent informed the user that the standard service fee is ninety-nine dollars, covering travel and diagnostics. The user declined further assistance and ended the call.

Pete Fisher called to follow up on his air conditioner and heater service as he had not received a callback. The agent acknowledged the issue and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The call was very brief and disjointed, with the agent attempting to start the conversation but the user responding with unclear or automated phrases. The call ended abruptly without any meaningful interaction or task completion.
The user called and asked if this was an answering service. The agent confirmed and explained that they would take down the user's details for follow-up.

The user called to speak with Dan Lagars and requested to leave a message for him. The agent confirmed the user's ZIP code and asked how to assist, but the user only wanted to leave a message. The call ended without further interaction.

The user, Kelly Johnson, reported a leaking water heater and requested a service appointment. The agent confirmed details, clarified the email address, and successfully booked a plumbing appointment for Tuesday, May 26, 2026, at 2 PM. The user declined to provide feedback but was satisfied with the booking process.

The user called Budget and inquired about the next steps after receiving an estimate related to the HSA. The agent acknowledged the query and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called Budget and provided two ZIP codes, but the first was not in the service area. The user then requested to speak with Dan Lagarth, but the call ended shortly after without further interaction.

Antoinette Moore called to schedule an AC checkup as a renter at 539 Dornoch Drive, Saint Louis, Missouri. The agent confirmed the details, offered available time slots, and successfully booked the appointment for May 25th at 12 PM. The user provided feedback rating the experience as a four and suggested the agent slow down a bit during the conversation.
The user called to return a call to Nicole but reached an AI agent named Sophie. The AI agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to complain about a plumbing issue involving confusion between Budget and American Home Shield services. The user expressed frustration over a plumber who allegedly caused more problems and charged extra money. The agent acknowledged the complaint and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to cancel their appointment scheduled for Wednesday, May 27, 2026. The agent confirmed the cancellation request multiple times and assured the user that the cancellation would be processed and followed up by the team. The call ended with the user being transferred to a live representative as requested.

Rick Lang called to schedule a technician visit to assess and recharge his cooling system due to a possible refrigerant leak identified during a previous semiannual visit. The appointment was successfully booked for May 27th at 12 PM. The agent confirmed the details and informed Rick about the dispatch fee and upcoming confirmation call.

The user called to inquire about the results of refrigerant line work done on their AC unit. The agent informed the user that they would pass the request to the team for a follow-up with the results and any next steps. The call ended with the user satisfied and no further requests.
